commit.message:
fix: move find_sibling_token to config_helpers module

Mypy in server CI rejects 'from provider.__init__ import' as it
registers __init__ as a separate module name. Moving to a dedicated
config_helpers module gives a clean 'from provider.config_helpers import'
path that the sync-to-fork sed rule rewrites correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Drop Python 3.9 support, require Python 3.10+ (#36)

Raises the minimum supported Python version to 3.10 across the project
metadata and CI/CD workflows.

## Changes

- **`pyproject.toml`**: Added `requires-python = ">=3.10"`
- **`ci.yml`**: Removed `"3.9"` from the test matrix
- **`cd.yml`**: Pinned `python-version` from `3.x` to `3.10`

Co-authored-by: shenxianpeng <3353385+shenxianpeng@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: GIF drag snapping + editable start/end inputs

Drag snapping fix (Timeline.vue):
- Move ALL pointer handling onto the track div itself instead of
  child handle elements. This eliminates the race condition where
  a pointerup on a handle caused a synthetic click event to fire
  on the track and reset the range back to 5s.
- Handle hit detection via HANDLE_HIT_PX (18px) proximity check in
  onTrackPointerDown — no child event listeners needed at all.
- Pointer capture on the TRACK element, so pointermove always arrives
  even when cursor moves outside the timeline bounds.
- Plain (non-reactive) dragMoved flag guards onTrackClick after a drag.
- Handle divs are now pointer-events-none (visual only).

Editable range inputs (GifControls.vue):
- Add start/end number inputs (step 0.1s) so users can type exact times
- Inputs are shown when a range is selected, above the quality buttons
- End input max is clamped to start + MAX_GIF_DURATION (10s)
- Duration badge shows current length and turns amber at the 10s limit
- Pass start, end, videoDuration props from editor.vue

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: serverless document processing pipeline (S3 → Lambda → Textract → DynamoDB → SNS)

- AWS SAM template with S3 bucket, DynamoDB tables, SNS topic, and 4 Lambda functions
- ProcessDocument: S3 trigger starts Textract OCR analysis
- StoreResults: persists extracted text to DynamoDB
- GetDocument/ListDocuments: REST API via API Gateway
- IAM least-privilege policies per function
- Unit tests with pytest
- Infrastructure-as-code with samconfig.toml

repository.name: intelligent-doc-engine
repository.description: Serverless document processing pipeline — S3 → Lambda → AWS Textract → DynamoDB → SNS. Built with AWS SAM, IAM least-privilege, and event-driven architecture.

commit.message:
fix: address PR #3614 review comments

- _resolve_token: treat all refresh errors (including LoginFailed) as
  non-fatal when stored token is available — prevents transient Passport
  failures from blocking startup
- _send: mark disconnected and schedule reconnect on send failure
  instead of silently swallowing the error
- _find_sibling_token: return None instead of empty string for missing
  x_token
- _build_device_dict: remove hardcoded volume_info and volume_granularity
  since volume sync was removed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Playwright: use standard browser path, add node-based CLI install

The PlaywrightRunner's browser install was failing because:
1. The pwsh script approach was broken (missing DLL)
2. The custom browsers path didn't check the standard ms-playwright location

Fixes:
- GetPlaywrightBrowsersPath() now checks standard ms-playwright first
- EnsureBrowsersInstalledAsync() tries node-based CLI (most reliable)
- Added FindNodePlaywrightCli() to discover node/cli.js from build output

Tested: standalone Blazor app + Playwright test passes with screenshots
and video recording confirmed working.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
asteroids: 3D world-space foundation + chase camera + missing layers

- engine: add obj.world.x/y/z writable runtime paths for OBJ sprites
  (routed through behavior_runner -> materialization -> sprite_renderer)
- engine: add camera-source: scene to share a single SceneCamera3D
  across multiple OBJ sprites in the same scene
- engine: add Set2DCamera, SetCamera3DLookAt, SetCamera3DUp commands
  and corresponding world.set_camera_3d_look_at / set_camera_3d_up APIs
- engine: wire obj.world.x/y/z to object_translate_x/y/z in ObjRenderParams
- schema: add world-x/y/z fields to scene.schema.yaml sprite properties
- docs: update SCRIPTING-API.md with new writable paths and camera API
- docs: update ARCHITECTURE.md and AUTHORING.md with 3D camera-source info
- asteroids: add camera-source: scene to ship.yml (shared 3D camera)
- asteroids: set obj.world.x/y/z on ship each frame from orbital sn basis
- asteroids: behind-ship chase camera with inertial lag (cam_n / cam_f)
  including banking sway from yaw_rate
- asteroids: wire solar-scene3d-layer and planets-layer into scene.yml
  (were authored but missing from layers list)
- asteroids: fix solar-scene3d-layer z_index (-30 -> -52, behind planet)
- asteroids: fix planets-layer z_index (1 -> -35, behind game canvas)

Update the tests for renamed RPC capability types and document the new RawSessionEventData behavior in validate-events.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add network/tools config to docs continuous improvement agentic workflow (#207)

* Initial plan

* fix: add network and tools config to docs_continuous_improvement workflow

The docs_continuous_improvement_every_3_days.md was missing the
'network: defaults' and 'tools: { edit:, bash: true }' frontmatter
sections that are required for the Copilot agent to have file-editing
permissions and network access.

Without 'tools: edit:', the <allowed-paths> section in the generated
prompt was empty, preventing the agent from editing any documentation
files. Without 'network: defaults', network access would be undefined.

Also update frontmatter_hash in the .lock.yml to reflect the updated
frontmatter. Note: the exact hash algorithm used by gh-aw compile could
not be determined without the gh-aw binary; the hash has been updated
to SHA-256 of the raw frontmatter text. Recompile with 'gh aw compile'
to regenerate the canonical hash if needed.

The compiled lock file body is unchanged since both network: defaults
and tools: { edit:, bash: true } were already reflected in the
compiled output via --allow-all-paths and ['defaults'] allowed domains.

Co-authored-by: carlkidcrypto <27721404+carlkidcrypto@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: carlkidcrypto <27721404+carlkidcrypto@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v7 suite — final squeeze with pool_order=2 and fit_subsample

Build conv_cifar_v7 suite (9 configs) testing the two genuinely
untested dimensions remaining after 6 prior sweeps:

1. pool_order=2 (second-order pooling statistics): appends per-channel
   variance in each SPP spatial block, doubling the feature dimension.
   The quick sanity test shows 64.79% vs 52.18% (v6 quick) at 2k
   neurons — a dramatic improvement that suggests variance captures
   texture discrimination missed by mean pooling alone.  Test at
   20k (×1/×5/×10) and 30k ×5 with α=2e-2.

2. fit_subsample scaling: we've always used 10k images for PCA filter
   learning and center selection.  Test 20k to see if more diverse
   patches improve filter and center quality.  Test at 20k ×5 and
   30k ×5/×10.

Also includes 48×3 30k ×10 (scaling v5's best filter config with
v6's ×10 ensemble size) and a combined ord2 + sub=20k config.

v6 findings driving these choices:
- 78.25% new record (30k ×10 α=2e-2), +0.37% over v5
- K-means filters dramatically worse (-14%), PCA conclusively best
- Per-patch normalization broken for single models
- α=1.5e-2 < α=2e-2 at 30k — confirms 2e-2 optimal
- Plateau around 78% with all prior levers exhausted

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: GIF range selection on timeline track

Previously the range selector was a separate 24px bar below the timeline,
making it impossible to discover and interact with. The user could only set
the playhead position on the main timeline, not a GIF range.

Now:
- Range selection is integrated directly into Timeline.vue
- In GIF mode: clicking the track sets start + auto-end (start+5s)
- Two full-height teal handles are rendered ON the timeline itself
- Handles use pointer capture so dragging stays responsive even when
  the cursor moves fast outside the element
- Dark overlay masks areas outside the selection; turns amber at 10s limit
- Duration badge shows inside the selection window
- Hint text 'Click to start selecting a range' shown when nothing selected
- The separate GifRangeSelector bar below the timeline is removed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[WIP] Fix entity identity issues with ObjectId and upsert behavior (#53)

* Initial plan

* Changes before error encountered

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ythril-network/Ythril/sessions/ae9f6b9a-d34a-4050-b5a4-0cbc85aa2acc

Co-authored-by: MetalMMagic <118023869+MetalMMagic@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: entity identity model — UUID-only IDs, ID-based upsert, no ghost stubs from remember

Defect 1: All write paths now generate UUID v4 (never ObjectId). Supplied IDs
are validated against UUID v4 regex and rejected with 400 if invalid.

Defect 2: upsertEntity uses _id as identity. With id → update by _id (or
insert with that _id). Without id → always insert new record. Name is a
non-unique searchable label.

Defect 3: remember no longer auto-creates ghost type:entity stubs. Entity names
are resolved via findEntitiesByName (no type constraint). Unresolved names
produce warnings; multiple matches link to all and warn.

Also adjusts bulk_write endpoints from main merge to use new identity model.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ythril-network/Ythril/sessions/e8eb3515-1335-459b-992d-61fbe30b3cd4

Co-authored-by: MetalMMagic <118023869+MetalMMagic@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Replace custom director with Playwright test agents

Replaced the custom Copilot SDK + Playwright MCP director with
Playwright's official test agent framework:

- npx playwright init-agents (planner/generator/healer agents)
- seed.spec.ts — opens DemoGod, waits for splash + session ready
- specs/demogod-showcase.md — natural language test plan for the
  full UI showcase (models, agents, modes, capabilities, skills,
  prompting, multi-session, floating layout)
- playwright.config.ts — video recording, webServer auto-start
- .github/agents/ — Playwright agent definitions for VS Code

The agents turn natural language specs into Playwright test code,
execute with video recording, and self-heal when selectors break.

Usage:
  npm test                    # run all tests
  npm run test:headed         # run with visible browser
  npm run test:showcase       # run showcase tests with browser

Videos saved to test-results/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
